bbc.title: 'I just want to be able to sleep': Attacks in Iran rock cities and cut power
bbc.description: Iranians in Tehran and Karaj tell the BBC they are exhausted and struggling to sleep after 10 days of Israeli and US attacks.

bbc.title: Steve Rosenberg: Russia seeks diplomatic and economic gains from Iran war
bbc.description: President Putin pits himself as a potential mediator but that's not an easy sell, writes the BBC's Russia editor.

bbc.title: Georgia voters to pick replacement for Marjorie Taylor Greene in special election
bbc.description: The shadow of Greene, who resigned from Congress after a rift with the president, looms large over a crowded contest.

bbc.title: Two more connected to Iranian football team stay in Australia, BBC understands
bbc.description: It is understood most of the team left Australia on Tuesday night after losing their Sunday game in the Asian Cup.

bbc.title: Russia's deportation of Ukrainian children amounts to crime against humanity, UN says
bbc.description: Vladimir Putin's direct involvement in the forcible deportation of Ukrainian children has been visible from the outset, the UN says.

bbc.title: Zendaya's ring and Stella McCartney's horses - the sights and trends at Paris Fashion Week

bbc.title: Acclaimed Peruvian writer Alfredo Bryce Echenique dies aged 87
bbc.description: The author is best known for his novel A World for Julius, which chronicles the frivolous lives of the elite.

bbc.title: Syrian ex-colonel in court on crimes against humanity charge
bbc.description: Salem Michel Al-Salem, accused of crimes committed in Syria in 2011 and 2012, has been named for the first time.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts

bloomberg.title: Permian Tycoon’s Heir Seeks $2 Billion in Diamondback Share Sale
bloomberg.description: Diamondback Energy Inc.’s largest shareholder is seeking to raise as much as $1.94 billion in a sale of shares in the oil and natural gas company, according to people familiar with the matter.
